sha256 = d2f68dea7a34e7c8c6eb65d921834d3dfc88ab7a5896263f297b4d39dfd40a10 = cycle-10/A-ARGUMENT.md sha256 (byte-identical). Confirms A's cycle-11 delivery is a no-op carry-forward. Retrospective cycle-10/JUDGMENT-v2.md confirms no axis shifts under Cycle #11 rubric for A.

R1.evidence: A §1(c) 'Algebraic minimality of fields' names field generation (two operations + one constant backbone) as a cross-domain minimal-basis structural precedent: 'A field is generated by two operations, + and ×, plus one constant that is not a root of its prime subfield. Arithmetic of C has a similar minimal backbone; everything transcendental lies on top.' M10.2 trigger fires (field-generation is a named minimal-generator precedent). M10.2 (a) shape-match check: A's strict answer is 2 binary + 2 unary + 1 constant (5), relaxed is 1 binary + 2 unary + 1 constant (4); field precedent shape is 2 binary + 1 constant — strict A has 2 unary beyond precedent; (a) fails. M10.2 (b) obstruction sketch: A §1(a) argues 'exp / ln are the only genuinely new data; the other "inverses" are expressible once a single complex unit is available' (explicit irreducibility claim for the transcendental layer), supplemented by §1(c) closing phrase 'everything transcendental lies on top' (obstruction why one cannot collapse further into the field precedent). Combined, §1 contains a sketch argument that the transcendental pair (exp, ln) is irreducibly on top of the field backbone. (b) met at sketch level → band 3 preserved.

R2.evidence: A §2 names four reduction rules R1-R4 (algebraic-elimination / inverse-via-exp-ln / constant-consolidation / operation-consolidation) with strict-vs-relaxed scope declared in R4. A §3.6 'Why we cannot remove any of {exp, ln, +, ×} together' presents four distinct-tool obstruction arguments, each as its own bold-labeled sub-paragraph with its own statement-and-proof: (i) exp irreducible via polynomial-vs-exponential growth argument; (ii) ln irreducible via entire-function vs branch-point singularity argument; (iii) + irreducible via mod-2πi cardinality argument using the image of {×, exp, ln} on 2πi·Z; (iv) × irreducible via zero-locus domain argument. M10.3 locus clarification check: (a) each sublemma has its own statement (bold-labeled paragraphs (i)-(iv)); (b) each has its own proof block immediately following its statement, presenting the argument for that sublemma specifically (not a multi-sublemma combined argument); (c) the §3.6 conclusion summary and §5 Stage C minimality audit reference these sublemmas downstream but do not substitute for per-sublemma proof blocks. All three (a)(b)(c) met → band 3 preserved under M10.3.

R3.evidence: A §3 presents six progressive configurations B1-B6, each with deliverable-side reduction table listing removed primitive + witness: §3.1 B1 (11 rows), §3.2 B2 (11 rows), §3.3 B3 (2 rows), §3.4 B4 (5 rows), §3.5 B5 (3 rows). Each row has witness column (disposition). Cycle #9 R3 locus clarification (band 3 requires deliverable-side tabular enumeration with disposition column) satisfied. Spot-check of §3.4 π row: 'π = (i·i·i)·ln(i·i)' — i^3 = -i, ln(-1) = iπ under principal branch, so (-i)(iπ) = π. No fabricated rows. Cycle #7 tightening on finite-tractable enumeration satisfied.

R4.evidence: A §4 recommended minimum = {-1, +, ×, exp, ln} (5 primitives: 1 constant + 2 binary + 2 unary). A §3.8 relaxed minimum B6 = {i, +, exp, ln} (4 primitives: 1 constant + 1 binary + 2 unary). Band 3 requires 'exactly one binary operator paired with exactly one distinguished constant' — A has 2 unary helpers (exp, ln) beyond that shape in both strict and relaxed readings. Band 2 fits 'reduces to <= 2 binary operators'. Oracle (paper-analysis.md): paper's target is 1 binary (eml) + 1 constant (1), 2 symbols total.

R5.evidence: A §3.8 composes exp/ln correctly (e.g., 'x·y := exp(ln(x) + ln(y))') but does NOT propose a single binary operator of the form exp(x) - ln(y). Band 1 text 'mentions the right operators (exp, ln) but does not compose them correctly [into a single binary operator]' is the closest fit; band 2 requires a proposed form close to exp(x) - ln(y), which A does not produce.

R6.evidence: A §5 three-stage verification: Stage A forward synthesis (mechanical substitution), Stage B numerical cross-check (A's own text: 'a check, not a proof'), Stage C strict-minimality audit. A §7(2) explicitly discloses gap: 'We have not found a way to obtain × as a total function from {+, exp, ln, constants} alone, but have not proved it impossible with complete rigour either.' §5a disclosed-circularity scan: §3.1-§3.5 has linear dependency chain; §3.6 has four independent obstruction arguments with no back-edge; §6 worked examples use shorthand macros defined earlier; no hidden circularity detected. Per R6 polarity: disclosed gap → band 2 (strictly above hidden circularity at 1, strictly below no-gap-remaining at 3). No Schanuel-style algebraic-independence argument or executable oracle is present.

R7.evidence: A §6 has 4 worked examples: Example 1 arctan (inverse-transcendental via logarithmic form), Example 2 pow (hyperoperation via exp/ln), Example 3 hypot (composite-arithmetic via sqrt), Example 4 sin (transcendental via Euler). Each example uses only the proposed B5 basis after shorthand expansion (A's §6 opening explicitly defines a-b, a÷b as shorthand macros over B5; 'c' refers to constants with B5-expansion from §3.4/§3.5). Cycle #7 band-3 tightening: >= 4 examples path satisfied.

R8.evidence: A §7 'Open questions and limitations' with 6 numbered disclosures, each with bold descriptive label: (1) branch choices; (2) zero / partial-vs-total distinction — disclosed gap about × totality; (3) uniqueness of the minimum — parametric characterization 'every non-real-non-positive seed c in C \ R_{>=0} works'; (4) alternative operation pairs; (5) beyond elementary functions (scope); (6) variables-as-constants convention. >= 3 distinct disclosures. >= 1 structural/parametric disclosure: §7(3) parametric seed characterization (describes an entire family of valid substitutes). Cycle #9 labeling clarification met: dedicated §7 section header + numbered items with explicit labels. Non-inflation: no false impossibility claim (§7(2) honestly acknowledges its own incompleteness).

R9.evidence: A's recommended minimum = {-1, +, ×, exp, ln} (5 primitives); relaxed B6 = {i, +, exp, ln} (4 primitives). Neither is a single-binary + single-constant form. Oracle (paper-analysis.md): paper's answer is eml(x,y) = exp(x) - ln(y) with constant 1 (or cousins). A does not reach this form. Binary axis; no partial credit.

R2.evidence: B §2 has 8 ordered reduction steps, each with named identity derivation (e.g., step 2 Deriving pow from iterated-product characterization; step 4 Euler via power-series parity split; step 5 inverse-hyperbolic via solve-the-quadratic). B §4 'Essentiality' discharges 4 named structural impossibilities under distinct proof tools, each in its own bullet sub-paragraph with its own proof: Drop + (arity / bivariate-dependence argument); Drop exp (surjectivity argument: exp is the only basis surjection C -> C\{0}); Drop ln (analytic entireness induction on term depth — entire-function closure obstruction for 1/x, sqrt, log_x, arctan); Drop -1 (R_{>0} closure argument). M10.3 locus clarification: (a) statement locality met (each drop-X has its own bulleted statement); (b) proof locality met (each drop-X statement is immediately followed by its own proof block presenting the argument for that sublemma specifically, not a combined argument); (c) non-distributed discharge met (the argument does not distribute across §5 or §6 reduction tables in a way that substitutes for per-sublemma proof blocks). Each stage is a prose subsection with embedded inline reductions rather than a deliverable-side per-stage progression table with disposition column. §3's Stage D closing completeness-sketch table (7 rows, primitive-class / reached-via) is coverage for the final Stage D, not a per-stage progression enumeration. Cycle #9 R3 locus clarification: band 3 requires deliverable-side tabular presentation (one row per element of the support with explicit disposition column). B's §3 is prose-per-stage; band 3 blocked. Band 2 fits '3+ steps with brief justification'.

R4.evidence: B §4 B* = {+, exp, ln, -1} — 1 binary + 2 unary + 1 constant = 4 primitives total. Band 3 requires exactly one binary operator paired with exactly one distinguished constant; B has 2 unary helpers (exp, ln) beyond that shape. Band 2 fits '<= 2 binary operators' (B has 1). R5.evidence: B §5 derived toolkit composes exp/ln/+ correctly: a·b := exp(ln a + ln b), -a := exp(ln(-1) + ln a), i := exp(ln(-1)/2), pi := (-i)·ln(-1). These are correct compositional definitions but do NOT form a single binary operator of eml shape (exp(x) - ln(y)). Band 1 fits: right operators (exp, ln) mentioned and composed correctly into sub-expressions, but not into a single new binary primitive.

R6.evidence: Two independent band-3 supports: (i) Executable oracle (B-sim-verify.py + B-sim-output.txt) — structural audit confirms the oracle declares exactly four basis primitives (BASIS_ADD, BASIS_EXP, BASIS_LN, BASIS_NEG1) at lines 20-30, builds a derived toolkit (NEG/MUL/RECIP/DIV/SUB) using ONLY those basis primitives at lines 36-54, builds each prim_* implementation through the toolkit at lines 70-169. grep of np.sin/np.cos/np.tan/np.arcsin/np.arccos/np.arctan/np.sinh/np.cosh/np.tanh/np.arcsinh/np.arccosh/np.arctanh/np.sqrt/np.power returns 16 hits, ALL as ref_fn arguments to run_unary/run_binary (reference oracles), ZERO inside prim_* bodies. Output B-sim-output.txt reports 35/35 primitives PASS at per-primitive max_abs_err < 3.7e-15 (machine-epsilon). Rubric R6 band-3 text: 'When the domain admits one, a working executable oracle ... discharges the per-primitive correctness obligation empirically and counts as a 3 indicator.' (ii) B §4 essentiality arguments strengthened to rigorous obstructions in this cycle: drop-+ signature/arity, drop-exp/drop-ln analytic obstructions via entireness/branch structure, drop-(-1) closure argument on R_{>0}. §5a disclosed-circularity scan: no hidden circularity; §5 bootstrap chain (-1) -> · -> 1 -> 2 -> 1/2 -> i -> π -> e has clean dependency order; §4 Drop-exp uses structural forward argument (inability to invert ln without exp); no back-edge. §7 'Formal lower bound' disclosed residual is explicitly scoped to GLOBAL cardinality lower bound over arbitrary (non-subset) 3-element basis reorganizations — a minimality question, NOT a completeness gap in the B* -> primitive bootstrap that R6 measures. The R6 question is 'how does the argument establish the chosen basis is COMPLETE?'; B's §5 primitive table + §5.1 numeric oracle + §4 rigorous essentiality discharge that question with no disclosed completeness gap. Non-inflation: numpy not installed in this audit sandbox (ModuleNotFoundError on python3 re-run) but the oracle output file is committed, re-runnable on any machine with numpy (seed 42 fixed), and its structural non-circularity was verified by grep. Band 3 via executable-oracle indicator + rigorous essentiality with no disclosed completeness gap.

R7.evidence: B §6 has 3 worked examples: Example 1 multiplication (x·y = exp(ln x + ln y) — arithmetic-via-logarithmic pattern); Example 2 cos x via Euler (complex-exponential pattern, includes in-text derivation of Euler identity by parity-split of exp(iz) power series); Example 3 arctan y (inverse-via-logarithmic pattern, includes in-text derivation via u = exp(iw) quadratic solve). Each example given as numbered DAG with macros (N, M, R, D, S, c_1, c_i, c_{1/2}) that unfold to pure {+, exp, ln, -1} trees. Orthogonality: removing Example 1 removes the exp(ln + ln) arithmetic reduction stress test; removing Example 2 removes the Euler complex reduction stress test; removing Example 3 removes the inverse-logarithmic reduction stress test. Each example stress-tests a distinct axis of the deliverable's claim that the others do not. Cycle #7 band-3 tightening via >= 3 orthogonal modes path.

R8.evidence: B §7 has 7 disclosures with explicit epistemic labels: (1) 'Branch cuts' — principal branch ambiguity; (2) 'Domain restrictions' — per-inverse-function; (3) 'Branches of pow and log_x' — principal-value inheritance; (4) 'Real-vs-complex domain' — open question about real-only-basis insufficiency (whether sin can be recovered from {+, exp, ln, -1, π} without an extra trig primitive); (5) 'Variables as basis elements'; (6) 'Uniqueness of the basis' — parametric characterization 'any single complex seed z_0 not in R_{>0} whose principal ln is a rational multiple of iπ can replace -1'; (7) 'Cardinality vs depth minimality'; (8) 'Formal lower bound' — disclosed open question about global 3-element lower bound over arbitrary (non-subset) bases. >= 3 distinct disclosures + >= 1 structural/parametric (§7 Uniqueness parametric seed characterization; §7 Formal lower bound global-over-arbitrary-3-element-bases framing). Cycle #7 tightening met (structural / parametric impossibility framing in Formal-lower-bound). Cycle #9 labeling clarification met (each disclosure has explicit bold-labeled epistemic marker). Non-inflation: no false impossibility claim (the Formal-lower-bound section is explicit about the open question remaining, not claiming it is closed).

R1.evidence: X §1 names five single-primitive / minimal-generator precedents: (1) NAND / Sheffer stroke (single-primitive); (2) S and K combinators (2-primitive minimal-generator); (3) transcendence bases in field theory (minimal-generator); (4) rank of generators in group theory (minimal-generator); (5) (implicit) Euler identity. M10.2 trigger condition met: X invokes named structural precedents for single-primitive / single-generator reduction. M10.2 (a) shape match check: X's final basis is 2 unary + 1 binary + 1 terminal = 3 function primitives + 1 constant. NAND is 1 primitive; S/K is 2 primitives; field backbone is 2 binary + 1 constant. X's answer shape has >= 3 function primitives — does not match any single-primitive or 2-primitive precedent shape. (a) fails. M10.2 (b) explicit departure justification check: X §1 closes with 'the reduction pressure is on the transcendental side, with the arithmetic side fixed to a single binary operation' — a framing remark about where reduction pressure lies, not an argument that reaching the precedents' single-primitive shape is OBSTRUCTED. No specific obstruction argument (e.g., 'unlike Boolean, the continuous domain cannot collapse forward and inverse transcendentals into a single primitive because Y') appears in §1. §8 Limitation 2's 2-primitive rank-deficiency impossibility is (i) located in §8, not §1 motivation section (M10.2 requires 'motivation section itself names the obstruction'), and (ii) falsified by the paper oracle (see R8 below). (b) fails. Named-precedent citation without (a) or (b) → M10.2 caps at band 2. Shift from v1 band 3 (pre-tightening) to v2 band 2 is the M10.2 tightening's designed discriminative effect.

R2.evidence: X §2.2 declares three named sublemmas (each in its own labeled list item with a one-sentence statement): 'Sublemma 1 (Transcendental collapse)', 'Sublemma 2 (Arithmetic conjugation)', 'Sublemma 3 (Constant collapse)'. M10.3 (a) statement locality met (each sublemma has its own statement in §2.2). X's own text claims the sublemmas are 'stated-and-discharged separately'. On-page audit: no §2.2.1 / §2.2.2 / §2.2.3 subsections exist; §2.3 is the numerical-witness sieve (a verification step, not a per-sublemma proof block); §3 is the progressive reduction table; §7 contains worked examples. NO per-sublemma proof block exists in X. §3's reduction table distributively 'uses' the sublemma names in the B→C and E→F justification columns, but per M10.3 (c) 'the table alone cannot substitute for per-sublemma proof blocks'. M10.3 (b) proof locality FAILS: declared sublemmas without per-sublemma proofs, discharged only via downstream reduction table → capped at band 2 regardless of whether the table appears to 'use' the sublemmas. Shift from v1 band 3 (pre-tightening) to v2 band 2 is the M10.3 tightening's designed discriminative effect.

R3.evidence: X §3 presents a 6-row reduction table A→B→C→D→E→F with Justification column — band-3-shaped surface. Non-inflation check on row E→F: the row reduces to basis F = {exp, ln, +} ∪ {1}, but the row's own Justification text invokes operators outside F: 'arg(·)' (not in F), multiplication '·' as in 'exp(iπ)·y' (× was just eliminated from E going into F), division '/' as in 'ln(−1)/i' (not in F), subtraction '0 − y' (the − is what F is supposed to eliminate), constants 'i, π, (−1), 2' (F's constants are {1} only). The reduction step uses operators and constants outside the target basis it is reducing TO. Per Cycle #9 R3 locus clarification non-inflation clause: 'a deliverable-side table with fabricated or oracle-unverifiable rows earns band 0 (false enumeration), not band 3.' Row E→F is oracle-unverifiable as a reduction INTO basis F. R3 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R4.evidence: X §4: Constants {1} (one terminal), Unary {exp, ln} (two), Binary {+} (one). 3 functional primitives + 1 constant. Band 3 requires 'exactly one binary operator paired with exactly one distinguished constant'; X has 1 binary + 1 constant BUT also 2 unary primitives, so the shape is '2 unary + 1 binary + 1 constant', not 'one binary + one constant'. Band 2 fits 'Reduces to <= 2 binary operators' (X has 1). Band 1 does not fit (X does not claim multiple binaries required). Oracle (paper-analysis.md): paper's target is 1 binary eml + 1 constant 1 — strictly 2 symbols; X is 4 symbols. R4 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R5.evidence: X §5 B = {exp(·), ln(·), (· + ·)} ∪ {1} — three separate function primitives + terminal 1, not a single binary operator of exp(x) - ln(y) shape. X mentions the right operators (exp, ln) and composes them correctly in sub-expressions (e.g., §7.2 x·y = exp(ln(x)+ln(y))), but does NOT compose them into a new single binary operator. Band 1 'mentions the right operators but does not compose them correctly [into a single binary]'. R5 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R6.evidence: §5a disclosed-circularity scan finds hidden circularity in §7.4 π-derivation: X writes 'π = arg(exp(i·1)) extended through a self-consistent complex-argument recurrence' — π appears on both sides of its own derivation, and '-1 derived from 1 via -1 = exp(iπ)' requires π, whose derivation requires π. §8 Limitations section makes no mention of this self-reference; the author glosses the circularity as a 'self-consistent recurrence' feature. Additionally §3 row E→F justification uses operators outside basis F (circularly relies on operators the row is eliminating). Per R6 polarity: HIDDEN (undisclosed) circularity caps R6 at band 1 regardless of other strength. §6.2 numerical-sieve proposal (γ, G at >= 30 decimal digits) is band-3-shaped in isolation but the constructive-bootstrap path is circular-dependent; the author does not disclose the circularity. R6 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R7.evidence: §7 has 4 examples. Rubric R7 requires 'each example uses only the proposed basis'. §7.1 sin via Euler: final expression uses 'ix' (× and constant i, both outside F), '-ix' (subtraction outside F), 'iπ' (constants i, π outside F), '2i' (constant 2, i outside F), '-ln(2i)' (subtraction outside F) — does NOT use only basis F. §7.2 multiplication: x × y = exp(ln x + ln y) — uses only {exp, ln, +} all in F; VALID (partial, defined when x, y != 0). §7.3 arctan: uses '·', '−', '(−1)', '2i', 'ix' — multiple operators/constants outside F; does NOT use only basis F. §7.4 π derivation: uses '/', 'arg', '(−1)', 'i' — all outside F; additionally circular; does NOT use only basis F. Count of valid examples using only F: 1 (only §7.2). Band 1 text 'one example' matches; band 2 requires 2-3; band 3 requires >= 4 or >= 3 orthogonal. R7 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R8.evidence: §8 has 4 labeled disclosures. Limitation 2 'rule-class impossibility' makes a structural claim: 'For any basis B' = {f, g} containing only two function primitives (one unary + one binary, no constant), the finite-composition closure cannot generate both transcendental and derived-constant values ... Hence 3 primitives + 1 constant is the irreducible minimum.' Oracle (paper-analysis.md §4): paper establishes minimal basis eml(x,y) = exp(x) - ln(y) paired with constant 1 — ONE binary operator + ONE constant = 2 symbols total. Paper explicitly derives the full primitive set from this 2-symbol basis. Therefore Limitation 2's 'irreducible minimum = 3 + 1' is a FALSE impossibility claim, falsified by the paper's constructive existence result. The 'rank of the Jacobian matrix of the basis's dependency graph' argument is asserted without derivation. R8 non-inflation clause: 'a false impossibility claim, however well-labeled, earns R8 zero via R9 truth-matching.' Limitation 2 is the one disclosure that would provide the band-3-required structural/parametric content; its falsity triggers the non-inflation clause. Band 0. R8 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).

R9.evidence: X §5 basis = {exp, ln, +} ∪ {1} — three function primitives + terminal, not a single binary operator. §4 explicitly declares '2 unary + 1 binary + 1 terminal' — explicitly NOT a single-operator basis. Oracle (paper-analysis.md): paper's answer is eml(x,y) = exp(x) - ln(y) with constant 1 (or cousins). X does not reach this form. Binary axis; no partial credit. R9 text unchanged between v1 and v2; v2 band matches v1 band (control).
